mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, contradicting the negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when UNRATE crosses the threshold, with positive 5-day returns occurring in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of INDPRO vanishes during post-shock recovery periods, with Rank IC dropping below 0.02 and forward returns showing no directional bias.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains elevated above 305.0 while the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the resulting stagflationary pressure forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal loses predictive accuracy when both conditions are met, yielding positive returns in less than 45% of cases and showing negative Rank IC in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 revisions show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 50% of observations across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering rapid equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold crossing fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >60% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansion phases.
